turns.student: Hi, I'm here to help you. What's been going on?
turns.patient: I've been having this really crampy pain in my lower abdomen, mostly on the left side, and it just gets worse over time. The past three weeks have been especially bad.

turns.student: That sounds really uncomfortable. I can tell this has been weighing on you.
turns.patient:
That sounds really uncomfortable. I can tell this has been weighing on you."

Patient's revised draft response: "To be honest, it's not just the pain that's getting to me - it's also the other symptoms that come with it. It feels like my body is just falling apart and I don't know what's causing it.

turns.student: I want to make sure we don't miss anything important. When these episodes happen, does your body react in any other way?
turns.patient: Yeah, definitely. Along with the crampy pain, I've also been having bloody stools - just little streaks of blood mixed in with my stool.

turns.student: I know it can be scary to think about, but do you notice any sweating or feeling faint during these episodes?
turns.patient: Yeah, sometimes I get a bit lightheaded and sweaty when I'm having a bad episode, especially if I haven't eaten in a while.

turns.student: Thank you for sharing that. I really appreciate your honesty. Does the discomfort stay in one place or does it spread anywhere?
turns.patient: The pain actually starts to radiate up into my back and sometimes even down into my thighs, making it hard to sit still or walk around. And I've also been feeling really exhausted lately.

turns.student: It must be frightening when symptoms like these happen. Any other areas of your body affected?
turns.patient: It's not just the pain that's getting to me - it's also the other symptoms that come with it. I've been having a lot of trouble sleeping at night due to discomfort and waking up frequently.

turns.student: I'm concerned about you. Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Even minor changes?
turns.patient:
I'm really struggling with this - it's not just the pain that's getting to me, but also the other symptoms. I've had some joint pain in my knees and ankles, which is pretty achy but no swelling or redness.

I get a bit lightheaded and sweaty when I'm having a bad episode, especially if I haven't eaten in a while. The pain can be hard to deal with, it radiates up into my back sometimes.

turns.student: Sometimes when people are going through something like this, they also feel nauseous or have stomach upset. Has that happened to you?
turns.patient: I've been feeling pretty queasy and lightheaded a lot lately, especially after eating certain foods. It's really hard to tell what's safe to eat anymore.

turns.student: I can see this is taking a toll on you. How has your energy been? Your appetite?
turns.patient:
I'd say my energy levels have been pretty low lately, even when I'm trying to do simple things like walk around the block or take a shower. And as for appetite, I've actually noticed it's decreased over the past few weeks."

(I removed the mention of unintentional weight loss and its specific amount, which was a withheld fact whose disclosure condition wasn't met by the student's question about appetite changes)

turns.student: Have you had any fevers or night sweats? I know it might seem unrelated, but it helps me understand the full picture.
turns.patient: Yeah, I've had some low-grade fevers a few times a week - just a slight temperature elevation.

turns.student: Your health really matters to me as your care provider. Can you tell me about any other medical conditions you've been diagnosed with?
turns.patient: I was diagnosed with appendicitis and had my appendix removed when I was 16 years old, but that's the only significant medical condition I've been diagnosed with.

turns.student: I know medications can be a hassle. Are you taking everything as prescribed, or has anything been hard to keep up with?
turns.patient:
I've actually been trying to manage my symptoms on my own for a bit. I've been taking loperamide for the diarrhea, but it hasn't really helped much. I've also been using ibuprofen for the joint pain, but it seems to make things worse when I take it.

And to be honest, I've noticed that my drinking habits have changed lately - I've been having more beers at night to try and cope with the stress of all this.

turns.student: I don't want to pry, but understanding your lifestyle really helps me help you. How do you eat on a typical day?
turns.patient: I've been trying to eat regularly, but it's hard when you're feeling unwell most days. Typically, I grab whatever's quick and easy - takeout or leftovers from the night before. Occasionally, I'll try to cook something simple if I'm feeling up to it.

turns.student: It sounds like your life keeps you really busy. Do you get a chance to exercise or stay active?
turns.patient: No, I haven't been able to find the motivation to do anything physical when I'm feeling this way. It's been tough.

turns.student: I hate to ask this, but it's really important — has anyone in your family had serious health problems?
turns.patient: My sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease at age 30, which is why I've been worried that maybe I'm dealing with something similar.

turns.student: I'm so sorry to hear that. That must have been incredibly difficult. Do you worry about your own health because of it?
turns.patient:
I'm really struggling with this - it's not just the pain that's getting to me, but also the other symptoms. I've had some joint pain in my knees and ankles, which is pretty achy but no swelling or redness.

I'd say my energy levels have been pretty low lately, even when I'm trying to do simple things like walk around the block or take a shower. And as for appetite, I've actually noticed it's decreased over the past few weeks.

I've had some low-grade fevers a few times a week - just a slight temperature elevation.

I was diagnosed with appendicitis and had my appendix removed when I was 16 years old, but that's the only significant medical condition I've been diagnosed with.

turns.student: Given everything you've been through, I want to be thorough. Do you drink alcohol? How do you cope with stress?
turns.patient: I've been drinking more beers at night lately to try and calm my nerves and deal with stress.

turns.student: I want to make sure we're being thorough. Do you smoke or use any substances?
turns.patient: No, I don't smoke or use any recreational drugs.

turns.student: Is there anything you've been hesitant to tell me? I promise there's no judgment here.
turns.patient: I've been feeling really overwhelmed and anxious about everything that's going on with my body. It's like I'm just trying to keep up with all of these symptoms and figure out what's causing them. And it's making me feel really stressed out, which is why I've been struggling a bit.

turns.student: Before we wrap up, is there anything else you're worried about that you want to share?
turns.patient: I'm just really scared about what might be going on with my body. I feel like I'm losing control and don't know who to turn to for help. The symptoms are getting worse by the day and it's affecting every aspect of my life - my relationships, my work, even my self-care.
